mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, weak labor demand signals severe economic contraction, depressing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of months when industrial production rises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the predictive accuracy (Rank IC) remains below 0.02 across all out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust economic expansion, supporting higher corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the combined proxy effect shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate cash flows and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of the business cycle phase.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 90.0, severe manufacturing contraction signals an imminent recession, depressing earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold condition fails to maintain a Rank IC above 0.02 in out-of-sample validation.
